event: intentional overdose of colchicine (60 tablets of 0.6 mg)
treated with colchicine 0.6 mg twice daily
electrocardiography showed normal sinus rhythm without ST-T changes
transthoracic echocardiogram revealed normal biventricular function without valvular disease or pericardial effusion
sepsis from an intrabdominal source suspected
elevated whole blood colchicine level (17 ng/ml, 14 h after presentation)
elevated serum colchicine level (5.7 ng/ml, 30 h after presentation)
escalating doses of intravenous vasopressors (epinephrine, norepinephrine, vasopressin)
high-dose vitamin B12 for refractory shock
initial readings consistent with distributive shock
hemodynamics rapidly changed to acute onset cardiogenic shock with high filling pressures
transesophageal echocardiogram revealed new severe biventricular failure (LVEF 15%)
intravenous milrinone added for inotropic support
started on continuous renal replacement therapy (CRRT) for anuric renal failure
packed red blood cell transfusion with 10 U of PRBCs initiated
white blood cell count continued to fall
echocardiogram showed recovery of LVEF to 50% to 55%
lab reports of elevated colchicine levels reported on day 10
patient admitted to taking 60 tablets of colchicine as an intentional overdose
